mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack increases and signals weakening economic momentum, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production confirms resilient supply-side capacity and supports short-term equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns in greater than 70 percent of tightening cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger corporate earnings fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent month-over-month coincide with positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declining retail sales signal consumer exhaustion and impending recession, triggering sharp short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 70 percent of late-cycle observations.
